data.title: Primaris REIT: Buy Rating Backed by Multi‑Year Growth Runway, Redevelopment Upside, and Strong Balance Sheet
data.description:
Tom Callaghan, an analyst from BMO Capital, maintained the Buy rating on Primaris Real Estate Investment Trust. The associated price target is C$25.00.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Tom Callaghan has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Primaris REIT’s strong year‑to‑date share performance and his view that the units still offer meaningful upside. He highlights a multi‑year growth runway supported by embedded leasing gains, particularly from recently signed and future leases, as well as the profitable re‑tenanting and redevelopment of former Hudson’s Bay (HBC) space, which is expected to drive solid net operating income and funds from operations growth.

Callaghan also points to Primaris’s robust balance sheet, disciplined capital recycling, and land monetization potential as key supports for the valuation and future returns. He notes that significant excess land and non‑core assets provide optionality for value realization, while a seasoned management team and ongoing operational execution underpin confidence in sustained same‑property NOI growth and further unit price appreciation, despite acknowledged leasing and tenant‑mix risks.

In another report released yesterday, TD Cowen also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a C$24.00 price target.

PMZ.UN’s price has also changed dramatically for the past six months – from C$15.570 to C$22.270, which is a 43.03% increase.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: James Bales Maintains Buy Rating Despite Defence Digital Contract Loss, Cutting Price Target to A$21.30 While Highlighting Strong Upside and Resilient Growth
data.description:
James Bales, an analyst from Morgan Stanley, maintained the Buy rating on Objective Corporation Limited. The associated price target was lowered to A$21.30.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

James Bales has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, even after factoring in the Defence Digital Group contract non-renewal and the related downgrade of the price target to A$21.30 from its prior level. He appears to believe that, despite near-term pressure on FY26 annual recurring revenue and the A$12–17 million impact from the lost USP agreement, the core business model and long-term growth profile remain intact and compelling.

At the current share price of A$10.30, the implied upside to his revised target is still substantial, supporting a positive risk‑reward view on the stock. In addition, Bales likely views the company’s diversified customer base, resilient recurring revenue streams and margin profile as sufficient to absorb this contract setback while still delivering attractive returns for shareholders over time.

According to TipRanks, Bales is an analyst with an average return of -4.1% and a 36.25% success rate. Bales covers the Consumer Cyclical sector, focusing on stocks such as Flight Centre Travel Group Limited, WEB Travel Group, and Temple & Webster Group Ltd.

data.title: Morgan Stanley Keeps Neutral Stance on Circle Amid Open USD Competition, Reiterates $106 Price Target
data.description:
In a report released yesterday, James Faucette from Morgan Stanley maintained a Hold rating on Circle Internet Group, Inc. Class A, with a price target of $106.00.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

James Faucette has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to emerging competitive dynamics and governance structures. The launch of Open USD by a large consortium introduces a rival stablecoin that emphasizes shared governance and open participation, contrasting with Circle’s more centralized control over USDC, which could make Open USD appealing to major platforms seeking influence over standards.

He also notes that Open Standard’s embedded revenue‑sharing model, where partners directly benefit from reserve earnings, differs from Circle’s more negotiated, distribution‑driven economics and may shift partner loyalties over time. While Coinbase’s role in the new consortium does not immediately undermine its existing relationship with Circle, it raises strategic uncertainty, justifying a neutral stance and keeping the price target unchanged at $106, reflecting balanced upside and downside risks.

In another report released today, Susquehanna also initiated coverage with a Hold rating on the stock with a $69.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Meta’s AI and Cloud Strategy Seen as High-Return Engine, Supporting Buy Rating and Unchanged $825 Price Target
data.description:
Jefferies analyst Brent Thill maintained a Buy rating on Meta Platforms today and set a price target of $825.00.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Brent Thill has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors related to Meta’s evolving AI and cloud strategy. He argues that Meta’s move to commercialize its surplus computing capacity is a deliberate, high-return capital allocation decision rather than evidence of overbuilding, mirroring the early AWS model by turning unused infrastructure into an additional cash-generating engine that supports ongoing capex and ROIC expansion.

Thill also highlights that industry-wide AI infrastructure remains severely supply-constrained, with no credible signs of excess capacity, which supports favorable pricing and utilization dynamics for Meta. In his view, Meta’s investments in AI models, potential entry into cloud-like services, and continued reliance on best-in-class partners such as CoreWeave together reinforce a long-term strategic position that can unlock additional monetization and margin upside, justifying the unchanged $825 price target and Buy rating.

In another report released today, Mizuho Securities also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $835.00 price target.

data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy on MediciNova as Advancing Pipeline and Upcoming ALS Catalysts Support Unchanged $6 Price Target
data.description:
Analyst Jason McCarthy of Maxim Group reiterated a Buy rating on Medicinova, retaining the price target of $6.00.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Jason McCarthy has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including MediciNova’s advancing pipeline and approaching catalysts. He points to the completed Phase 2 trial of MN-001 in patients with hypertriglyceridemia, NAFLD, and type 2 diabetes, where upcoming data on liver fat and triglyceride reduction could validate its multi-pathway mechanism and prior signals of lipid improvement.

McCarthy also emphasizes the MN-166 ALS program, with the pivotal COMBAT-ALS Phase 2b/3 trial expected to deliver topline results by year-end 2026, potentially enabling an NDA submission in the first half of 2027. With additional real-world evidence coming from the SEANOBI expanded-access study and supportive prior safety and efficacy data, he views the overall risk/reward as attractive, maintaining his Buy rating and a $6 price target, which remains unchanged.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Shlomo Rosenbaum Lifts FactSet Price Target Modestly, Keeps Hold Rating as AI Upside Balances Valuation Risks
data.description:
Stifel Nicolaus analyst Shlomo Rosenbaum maintained a Hold rating on Factset Research today and set a price target of $254.00.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Shlomo Rosenbaum has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors related to FactSet’s growth profile and valuation. He views AI as a supportive force rather than a threat, with new AI offerings contributing to incremental Annual Subscription Value and helping accelerate growth, but still not large enough to materially alter the company’s long-term trajectory.

At the same time, Rosenbaum notes that FactSet’s shift toward more activity-based, consumption-driven pricing could create some gap between ASV and reported revenue, adding a layer of uncertainty to forecasting. He also highlights that future margin expansion will depend on how aggressively management reinvests in AI and other growth initiatives, leading him to lift his target price modestly while concluding that, at current levels, the risk‑reward profile is balanced rather than compelling for a more aggressive rating.

data.title: Scynexis: Buy Rating Reiterated as Rare Disease Pipeline Advances; $32 Price Target Maintained
data.description:
Jason McCarthy, an analyst from Maxim Group, maintained the Buy rating on SCYNEXIS. The associated price target remains the same with $32.00.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Jason McCarthy has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Scynexis’ strong financial position and emerging rare disease focus. He highlights that the company is funded into mid-2029 and is advancing SCY-770, an oral AMPK activator with encouraging preclinical data showing reduced cyst growth and favorable metabolic effects in autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease.

He also points to near-term clinical catalysts, such as Phase 1 pharmacokinetic data for SCY-770 and the Phase 1 readout for antifungal candidate SCY-247, both expected in 3Q26, which could increase investor interest as the rare disease strategy takes shape. In his valuation work, McCarthy incorporates risk-adjusted future revenues from SCY-770 and royalties from Brexafemme, and, applying a 25% discount rate across his cash flow and earnings models, maintains a 12-month price target of $32, supporting his Buy recommendation.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Buy Rating Reiterated on iBio as IBIO-610 Data Underscore Differentiated Efficacy; $5 Price Target Maintained
data.description:
Analyst Patrick Dolezal of LifeSci Capital maintained a Buy rating on Ibio, retaining the price target of $5.00.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Patrick Dolezal has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, starting with the exceptionally strong preclinical results for IBIO-610, which achieved nearly complete and durable activin E suppression in obese non-human primates after a single dose. He views this level of target engagement as highly differentiated versus competing INHBE-directed siRNA therapies, and believes it could translate into superior outcomes on weight loss and body composition measures in humans.

In addition, he highlights the encouraging combination data of IBIO-610 with semaglutide, where the regimen delivered greater reductions in visceral and total fat while meaningfully preserving lean mass compared with semaglutide alone. Coupled with prior translational evidence from other INHBE-targeting programs and a clear path toward an IND-equivalent filing for IBIO-610 in late 2026, he sees a compelling risk/reward profile that supports maintaining a Buy rating and a $5 price target.

Dolezal covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Neurogene, Oculis Holding, and MBX Biosciences, Inc.. According to TipRanks, Dolezal has an average return of 15.3% and a 42.86% success rate on recommended stocks.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
